{"step_by_step_thinking": "Reviewing the documents, there is no direct statement that any of the anesthetic agents listed are contraindicated in epileptic patients. However, considering the information provided, propofol has been associated with a higher risk of propofol infusion syndrome, which could be life-threatening in epileptic patients. Additionally, ketamine has been noted to potentially exacerbate seizures in some cases. Given the options, propofol seems to have the highest risk factor for complications in epileptic patients.", "answer_choice": "C"}
